mnemonics: LATCHES HOLD ONTO DATA: Like A Tiny Clamp, Holds Output 'til New Entry Sets
keyPoints: A latch is a level-sensitive memory element; its output depends on the current level of its enable signal (e.g., a clock).
Latches are transparent when enabled, meaning the output follows the input while the enable signal is active.
The most basic types are the SR Latch (Set-Reset) and the D Latch (Data), which stores a single bit.
commonMistakes: Confusing latches (level-sensitive) with flip-flops (edge-triggered); latches can cause timing problems in synchronous circuits if not used carefully.
Forgetting that an SR Latch can enter an invalid or metastable state when both S and R are asserted (S=1, R=1).
